channel.title: Homes on Stephen Berg
channel.description: Recent content in Homes on Stephen Berg

item.description: Biography I am an assistant professor of statistics at Penn State University. Here is a link to my cv.

item.title: Manuscripts
item.description: Berg, S., Zhu, J., Clayton, M.K. (2019) Control variates and Rao-Blackwellization for deterministic sweep sampling. Under revision. [arXiv] Berg, S., Zhu, J., Clayton, M.K., Shea, M.E., Mladenoff, D.J. (2019) A latent discrete Markov random field approach to identifying and classifying historical forest communities based on spatial multivariate tree species counts. Annals of Applied Statistics, vol. 13 no. 4, pp. 2312–2340. [pdf] Fernandes-Taylor, S., Berg, S., Gunter, R., Bennett, K.

item.title: Software
item.description: GitHub The automultinomial package, for regression models (similar to logistic regression) of spatially correlated discrete data. Vignette with detailed description and guide to use. Available for download on cran and GitHub The bcd package. This is a C++ implementation (with R wrappers via Rcpp) of block coordinate descent for fitting group lasso penalized linear (logistic, multinomial, Poisson, multiresponse linear) regression models. It supports the overlapping group lasso without explicit duplication of the columns of the design matrix, and sparse design matrices.

item.title: Teaching
item.description: +Graduate student lecturer, Statistics 571 - Statistics for Bioscience I (Fall 2018, Fall 2019) +TA, Statistics 301 - Introduction to Statistical Methods (Spring 2015) +TA, Statistics 224 - Introductory Statistics for Engineers (Fall 2014)

item.title: Presentations
item.description: Workshop on Rcpp/RcppEigen for student seminar, UW-Madison, 12/7/2018 (slides) (materials.zip)
